mission.title: Install and Configure Artifact Hub on Kubernetes
mission.description: Artifact Hub is a web-based application that enables finding, installing, and publishing Cloud Native packages. Installing it allows you to easily discover and manage various artifacts for your CNCF projects in a centralized manner.

steps.title: Add the Bitnami Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Bitnami repository which contains the PostgreSQL dependency needed for Artifact Hub.
```bash
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Artifact Hub using Helm
steps.description:
Install Artifact Hub with the specified version in a new namespace called 'artifact-hub'.
```bash
helm install artifact-hub artifact-hub/artifact-hub --version 1.22.1-3 --namespace artifact-hub --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Artifact Hub pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace artifact-hub
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the values.yaml file to set resource limits for the Artifact Hub deployment. You can use the following command to set limits directly:
```bash
helm upgrade artifact-hub artifact-hub/artifact-hub --namespace artifact-hub --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi
```

steps.title: Access the Artifact Hub
steps.description:
Port-forward to access the Artifact Hub UI locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/artifact-hub --namespace artifact-hub 8080:80
```

steps.title: Open Artifact Hub in your browser
steps.description: Open your web browser and navigate to http://localhost:8080 to access the Artifact Hub interface.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Artifact Hub pods in a Running state when you execute the `kubectl get pods` command. You should also be able to access the Artifact Hub UI at http://localhost:8080.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update
helm upgrade artifact-hub artifact-hub/artifact-hub --namespace artifact-hub --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed on your local machine.
